[1160]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兰台又言 - Formal phrase meaning 'the LanTai reported again,' indicating a continuation of previous reports or observations.
  • 尝晴昼仰视 - Phrase meaning 'once on a clear daytime, looking up,' describing the time and action of the observation.
  • 头角略与画图同 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the head and horns were roughly the same as those in the drawings,' comparing the observed creature to known depictions.
  • 四足开张，摇撼如一舟之鼓四棹 - Vivid metaphor meaning 'four legs spread out, shaking like a boat rowing with four oars,' describing the movement of the creature.
  • 尾匾而阔，至末渐纤 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the tail was flat and broad, gradually becoming thinner at the end,' detailing the shape of the tail.
  • 在似蛇似鱼之间 - Comparative phrase meaning 'between the likeness of a snake and a fish,' emphasizing the creature's ambiguous nature.
  • 腹下正白如匹练 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the underside was pure white like a ribbon,' highlighting the color and texture of the creature's belly.
  • 阴雨见龙 - Phrase meaning 'seeing a dragon in rainy weather,' referring to common folklore or historical accounts of dragons appearing in storms.
  • 或露首尾鳞爪耳 - Phrase meaning 'sometimes only the head, tail, scales, and claws were visible,' describing the partial visibility of dragons in such conditions.
  • 未有天无纤翳，不风不雨，不电不雷 - Phrase meaning 'there has never been a time when the sky was completely clear, without wind, rain, lightning, or thunder,' emphasizing the unusual nature of the sighting.
  • 视之如此其明者 - Phrase meaning 'seen with such clarity,' highlighting the distinct and clear nature of the observation.
  • 录之亦足资博物也 - Concluding phrase meaning 'recording it is also sufficient to contribute to the study of natural phenomena,' indicating the value of documenting the sighting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兰台 - Refers to the LanTai, an ancient Chinese imperial library and archive, known for its role in preserving and recording historical and cultural information.
  • 龙 - A mythical creature in Chinese culture, often associated with power, authority, and natural phenomena such as rain and storms.
  • 阴雨 - Refers to rainy or stormy weather, often linked to the appearance of dragons in Chinese folklore.
  • 博物 - Refers to the study of natural history and natural phenomena, emphasizing the importance of documenting and studying unusual observations.

Proper Nouns

  • 兰台 - The LanTai, an ancient Chinese imperial library and archive.
  • 龙 - The dragon, a mythical creature in Chinese culture.

[1161]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 趙鹿泉前辈言 - Narrative formula meaning 'Master Zhao Lüquan, the elder, said,' introducing a story or anecdote.
  • 未第时 - Classical expression meaning 'before passing the imperial examinations' or 'before being a scholar-official.'
  • 馆于某家 - 'Taught as a tutor in a certain family,' referring to a traditional role of a scholar who served as a private tutor.
  • 主人之母适病危 - 'The master’s mother had just fallen critically ill.' The phrase *适* here is used to indicate a recent occurrence.
  • 馆童具晚餐 - 'The houseboy had prepared dinner.' *馆童* refers to a servant or student assistant in a scholar’s household.
  • 命置别室几上 - 'Ordered to place it on a table in another room.'
  • 倏见一白衣人入室内 - 'Suddenly saw a white-robed figure enter the room.' *倏* means 'suddenly' or 'in a flash.'
  • 方恍惚错愕 - 'Was in a state of confusion and shock.'
  • 逡巡入 - 'Hesitantly entered,' suggesting hesitation or caution.
  • 厉声叱之 - 'Shouted at them sternly.'
  • 遁去 - 'Fled away.'
  • 匿于墙隅 - 'Hid in the corner of the wall.'
  • 坐于户外观其变 - 'Sat outside the door to observe the situation.'
  • 踉跄出 - 'Staggered out,' suggesting the person was weak or in distress.
  • 顷病者作鬼语 - 'The patient just now spoke in the language of the dead.' *顷* means 'just now' or 'recently.'
  • 称冥使奉牒来拘 - 'Claimed that the messengers of the Underworld had come with a summons to take him away.' *冥使* refers to officials of the afterlife, *牒* is an official document or summons.
  • 其一为先生所扼不得出 - 'One of them was being held back by the gentleman and could not escape.' *扼* means to seize or block.
  • 恐误程限 - 'Fearing that they would miss the time limit.'
  • 使亡人获大咎 - 'Causing the deceased to suffer great punishment.'
  • 未审真伪 - 'Uncertain whether it was true or false.'
  • 先生乃移坐他处 - 'The gentleman then moved to sit elsewhere.'
  • 仿佛见黑衣短人狼狈去 - 'Faintly saw the black-robed short figure flee in disarray.'
  • 内寝哭声如沸矣 - 'Inside the inner chamber, the sound of weeping was like boiling water.' A metaphor for intense crying.
  • 先生笃实君子 - 'The gentleman was a sincere and virtuous person.'
  • 一生未尝有妄语 - 'Never spoke a falsehood in his entire life.'
  • 此事当实有也 - 'This event must have truly occurred.'
  • 阴律至严 - 'The laws of the Underworld are extremely strict.'
  • 神听至聪 - 'The gods’ hearing is extremely sharp.'
  • 摄魂吏卒 - 'Soul-capturing officials and attendants,' referring to the officials of the afterlife who collect souls.
  • 不免攘夺病家酒食 - 'Could not help but seize the food and drink of the sick family.' *攘夺* means to seize or plunder.
  • 然则人世之吏卒，其可不严察乎？ - 'Then, how could the officials and attendants of this world fail to be rigorously examined?' A rhetorical question emphasizing the need for vigilance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 赵鹿泉前辈 - Refers to a scholar or elder named Zhao Lüquan, likely a real historical figure or a representative of a scholarly tradition.
  • 孙虚船先生 - Refers to Mr. Sun Xuchuan, likely a scholar or writer of the Qing or earlier dynasties, known for his literary or moral writings.
  • 馆于某家 - A common practice in pre-modern China where scholars who had not yet passed the imperial examinations served as private tutors in wealthy families.
  • 白衣人 - A common representation of a ghost or spirit in Chinese folklore, often associated with the dead or supernatural beings.
  • 黑衣短人 - Likely refers to a demon or spirit, or a supernatural being of the underworld, given its description and behavior.
  • 冥使 - 'Officials of the Underworld,' a term from Chinese religious and folk beliefs, referring to the agents of the afterlife who carry out the will of the underworld.
  • 牒 - A document or summons used in both earthly and underworld bureaucracy, similar to a warrant or official notice.
  • 摄魂吏卒 - Officials or attendants in the underworld responsible for capturing souls. This reflects the belief in a bureaucratic afterlife, common in Chinese religious traditions such as Daoism and Confucian cosmology.
  • 阴律 - 'Laws of the Underworld,' referring to the strict moral and legal codes believed to govern the afterlife.
  • 神听至聪 - 'The gods’ hearing is extremely sharp,' reflecting the belief in divine omniscience and the importance of moral behavior.
  • 攘夺病家酒食 - The act of stealing food and drink from the sick family by supernatural beings, which is a common motif in Chinese ghost stories and folktales.

Proper Nouns

  • 赵鹿泉前辈 - 'Master Zhao Lüquan, the elder' – A proper name of a scholar or storyteller, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 孙虚船先生 - 'Mr. Sun Xuchuan' – A proper name of a scholar or writer.

[1162]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 门人伊比部秉绶言 - Classical narrative opening meaning 'A disciple named Yi Bibo Bingshou said.' Common in classical Chinese storytelling to introduce a tale.
  • 赴京应试 - To go to the capital for the imperial examination, a key rite of passage for scholars.
  • 寓西河沿旅舍中 - Stayed at a lodging house by the Western Riverbank. 西河沿 is a proper place name, likely referring to a location near a river in the capital, possibly Beijing.
  • 壁悬仕女一轴 - A scroll of a courtesan or lady was hung on the wall. 仕女 refers to a courtesan or a refined woman in classical literature.
  • 风姿艳逸，意态如生 - Her appearance was graceful and alluring, her expression as if alive. Poetic description of the woman in the painting, emphasizing her lifelike quality.
  • 每独坐辄注视凝思 - Whenever he sat alone, he would gaze at it in deep thought. 辄 is a classical particle meaning 'would always' or 'would often.'
  • 客至或不觉 - When guests came, he sometimes did not notice. 或 here is used to indicate frequency or possibility.
  • 翩然自画下 - She suddenly appeared from the painting, gracefully. 翩然 is a poetic term for graceful movement.
  • 宛一好女子也 - She was indeed a beautiful woman. 宛 is used to emphasize that the transformation was real and unmistakable.
  • 书生虽知为魅，而结念既久，意不自持 - Although the scholar knew she was a spirit or demon, his thoughts had been fixed on her for so long that he could not restrain himself. 魅 refers to a spirit or demon, often in the context of fox spirits or other supernatural beings.
  • 遂相与笑语燕婉 - So they conversed and laughed in a tender and harmonious manner. 燕婉 is a classical term for gentle, affectionate speech.
  • 下第南归 - After failing the examination, he returned south. 下第 is a term used to describe failing the imperial examination.
  • 竟买此画去 - He ended up buying this painting and taking it home. 竟 indicates a surprising or unexpected result.
  • 至家悬至书斋，寂无灵响 - When he brought it home and hung it in his study, there was no further supernatural activity. 寂无灵响 means 'no more signs of spirit or supernatural presence.'
  • 真真之唤弗辍也 - The voice of 'Zhenzhen' did not cease. 真真 is a term often used in Chinese literature to refer to a supernatural female spirit or a beloved name, suggesting a personal connection.
  • 三四月后，忽又翩然下 - Three or four months later, she suddenly appeared again. 忽 indicates suddenness, a common classical marker.
  • 与话旧事不甚答 - She spoke of old matters, but did not answer much. 不甚答 means 'did not respond much' or 'did not elaborate.'
  • 亦不暇致诘，但相悲喜 - He had no time to question her, but only shared sorrow and joy. 不暇致诘 is a phrase indicating lack of time or opportunity to ask questions.
  • 自此狎媟无间 - From then on, they became intimately familiar with each other. 狎媟无间 is a term indicating close, intimate, and possibly inappropriate familiarity.
  • 遂患羸疾 - He then fell ill with a wasting disease. 羸疾 refers to a wasting or debilitating illness.
  • 其父召茅山道士劾治 - His father summoned a Taoist priest from Mount Mao to exorcise the spirit. 茅山 refers to Mount Mao, a famous Taoist mountain in Jiangsu, associated with Taoist exorcism and ritual.
  • 道士熟视壁上，曰：画无妖气，为祟者非此也 - The Taoist priest looked closely at the wall and said: 'The painting has no demonic energy; the one causing the disturbance is not this one.' 妖气 is 'demonic energy' or 'spiritual corruption.'
  • 结坛作法 - Set up a ritual circle and performed the exorcism. 结坛 is a term for setting up a ritual space.
  • 次日有一狐殪坛下 - The next day, a fox died beneath the ritual circle. 殪 means to die suddenly or be killed.
  • 知先有邪心，以邪召邪，狐故得而假借 - He knew that the scholar had already harbored impure desires, and thus summoned a demon with his own impurity, allowing the fox to take advantage. 邪心 is 'impure heart' or 'evil intention,' and 假借 is 'to borrow' or 'to use as a vessel.'
  • 其京师之所遇，当亦别一狐也 - The fox he met in the capital was likely another one. This suggests that the scholar had previously been involved with another fox spirit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 门人 - Disciple or student, a common term in classical Chinese for a follower of a teacher or scholar.
  • 伊比部秉绶 - Proper name: Yi Bibo Bingshou. 伊 is a common prefix for names, 比部 may refer to a bureaucratic office or title, and 秉绶 is a surname. The name is likely fictional or stylized.
  • 仕女 - A term for courtesans or refined women, often used in classical Chinese art and literature.
  • 魅 - A supernatural being, often a fox spirit (hu), which is a common motif in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 真真 - A name or term used to refer to a supernatural female spirit. It may be a name or a poetic term for a beloved or enchanting spirit.
  • 茅山道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest from Mount Mao, a famous center of Taoist exorcism and ritual in Jiangsu province.
  • 狐 - Fox in Chinese folklore is often associated with spirits or demons, particularly hu (fox spirits), which can take human form and interact with humans, often with ambiguous or dangerous intentions.

  • 使功成事定之後，殁于战阵者，皆挟以为仇 - 如果那些在成功後戰場上死去的人，都懷恨在心，是一個假設的情況，暗示所有戰死的士兵都會尋仇。
  • 古来名将，无不为鬼所殛矣 - 古代名將都會被鬼魂擊殺，是一種誇張的說法，強調前一情景的荒謬性。
  • 有是理乎 - 這合理嗎？是一種修辭問句，質疑前一說法的邏輯。
  • 且颜良受歼已久，越一二千年，曾无灵响 - 而且顏良已經被殺很久了，超過一千年，沒有任何靈異現象，指顏良的鬼魂未出現。
  • 何忽今日而为神，何乎今日而报怨 - 為什麼他今天突然變成神，為什麼今天要報仇，質疑顏良鬼魂現身的合理性。
  • 揆以天理，殆必不然 - 根據天理，這肯定不會發生，斷言前一情景的不可能性。
  • 是盖庙祝师巫，造为诡语，山妖水怪，因民所荧惑而依砶之 - 這可能是廟祝和巫師編造的奇異故事，山精水怪利用百姓的迷惑而依附其上，暗示靈異現象的真實原因。
  • 刘敬叔异苑曰 - 劉敬叔的《異苑》說，引述劉敬叔的《異苑》這部奇異故事集。
  • 丹阳县有袁双庙，真第四子也 - 丹陽縣有袁雙廟，真第四子，介紹袁雙廟及其供奉的袁真第四子。
  • 真为桓宣武诛，便失所在 - 袁真被桓宣武殺死，袁雙就消失了，解釋袁雙消失的原因。
  • 太元中形见于丹阳，求立庙，未即就功 - 太元年間袁雙在丹陽現身，請求立廟，但未立即建成，描述袁雙的現身和請求。
  • 大有虎灾，被害之家辄梦双至，催功甚急 - 有許多老虎災害，受害的家庭常常夢見袁雙到來，催促建廟非常急切，解釋建廟的急迫性。
  • 百姓立祠，于是猛暴用息 - 百姓建立了祠堂，於是猛獸的暴行停止了，顯示祠堂的效果。
  • 常以二月晦，鼓舞祈祠，其日恒风雨 - 總是二月的最後一天，在祠堂前舉行祈禱儀式，那天總是風雨交加，描述與祠堂相關的儀式。
  • 至元嘉五年，设奠讫，村人邱都于庙后见一物，人面鼍身，葛巾，七孔端正而有酒气 - 到元嘉年間第五年，設奠完畢，村民邱都在廟後見到一個東西，人面鼉身，戴著葛巾，七個孔洞端正且有酒氣，描述一次超自然遭遇。
  • 未知为双之神，为是物凭也 - 不知道是袁雙的神靈，還是被物體附身，表達對遭遇性質的不確定。
  • 余谓来必风雨，其为水怪无疑 - 我認為如果來時有風雨，那一定是水怪無疑，表達對遭遇性質的個人看法。
  • 然则是事古有之矣 - 然而這些事情自古就有，總結類似現象在過去也存在。

  • The long sentence '昌吉守備劉德言，昔征回部時，因有急檄，取珠爾土斯路弛往，陰晦失道，十餘騎皆迷，裹糧垂盡，又無水泉，姑坐樹根，冀天晴辨南北。' describes a series of events in a compact form.

  • 自深州病家归 - Returned from a patient's home in Shen Zhou.
  • 沿途寻衅 - Sought trouble along the way.
  • 无一日不与人竞也 - There was no day he did not quarrel with someone.
  • 昏夜至一村 - At dusk, he arrived at a village.
  • 旅舍皆满 - All the inns were full.
  • 乃投一寺 - So he went to a temple.
  • 惟佛殿后空屋三楹 - There is only a vacant room behind the Buddha hall.
  • 有物为祟 - Something that haunts or causes trouble.
  • 人定后果有声呜呜自外入 - After the hour of man, a sound like 'wo-wo' came from outside.
  • 乃一丽妇也 - It was a beautiful woman.
  • 渐逼近榻 - She gradually approached the bed.
  • 杨突起拥抱之 - Yang suddenly rose to embrace her.
  • 即与接唇狎戏 - Kissing her and playing with her intimately.
  • 妇忽现缢鬼形 - The woman suddenly appeared as a hanged ghost.
  • 恶状可畏 - With a terrifying appearance.
  • 赞庵战栗 - Zan An trembled.
  • 齿相击 - Teeth chattering.
  • 杨徐笑曰 - Yang calmly laughed and said.
  • 汝貌虽可憎 - Though your appearance is repulsive.
  • 下体当不异人 - Your lower body should not be different from a human.
  • 且一行乐耳 - And let's have some fun!
  • 左手揽其背 - He held her back with his left hand.
  • 右手遽褪其瞯 - Quickly pulled down her pants with his right hand.
  • 将按置榻上 - Intending to place her on the bed.
  • 鬼大号逃去 - The ghost screamed and fled.
  • 杨追呼之 - Yang chased and called after her.
  • 竟不返矣 - But she did not return.
  • 遂安寝至晓 - He then slept peacefully until dawn.
  • 临行语寺僧曰 - As he was leaving, he told the monk.
  • 此屋大有佳处 - This room is very good.
  • 吾某日还 - I will come back on a certain day.
  • 当再宿 - And stay again.
  • 勿留他客也 - Do not let other guests stay here!
  • 赞庵尝以语沧州王友三曰 - Zan An once told Wang You San of Cangzhou.
  • 世乃有逼奸缢鬼者 - The world has people who forcibly have sex with hanged ghosts.
  • 横虎之名 - The name 'horizontal tiger'.
  • 定非虚得 - Is definitely not empty of meaning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 疡医 - Refers to traditional Chinese medicine practitioners who specialized in treating skin diseases or wounds.
  • 深州 - Refers to Shen Zhou, a place in ancient China.
  • 杨姓仆 - Refers to an attendant named Yang, where '杨' is a surname.
  • 横虎 - A metaphor for someone who is extremely violent or aggressive.
  • 佛殿后空屋三楹 - Refers to a vacant room behind the Buddha hall in a temple, where '三楹' means three rooms.
  • 有物为祟 - Refers to something that haunts or causes trouble, a common belief in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 人定 - Refers to the hour of man, a traditional Chinese time division, typically around 9 PM to 11 PM.
  • 缢鬼 - Refers to a hanged ghost, a type of ghost in traditional Chinese folklore.
  • 沧州王友三 - Refers to Wang You San of Cangzhou, a person in the story.

[1216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 科场为国家取人材 - Formal expression meaning 'the examination hall is for the state to select talents,' emphasizing the public and official purpose of the imperial examination system.
  • 非为试官取门生也 - Means 'not for the examiners to take disciples,' highlighting that the primary goal is not personal gain but state service.
  • 分卷之 - Means 'to divide the exam papers,' referring to the practice of distributing exam papers among different examiners.
  • 美恶则无定 - Means 'good or bad is not fixed,' indicating the subjective nature of evaluating exam papers.
  • 有拨房之例 - Means 'there is a practice of reallocating exam papers,' referring to the custom of moving papers between examiners to ensure fairness.
  • 杨丈不以介意 - Means 'Mr. Yang did not take it to heart,' indicating his indifference to the reallocation.
  • 诸卷实胜我房卷 - Means 'the papers in other rooms are indeed better than mine,' showing his acknowledgment of the quality of other papers.
  • 不敢心存畛域 - Means 'I dare not harbor prejudices,' emphasizing his impartiality.
  • 使黑白倒置也 - Means 'to make black and white inverted,' referring to the potential for unfairness if papers are not evaluated fairly.
  • 闻之座师介野园先生 - Means 'I heard it from my mentor, Mr. Jie Yayuan,' indicating the source of the information.
  • 先生即拨入杨丈房者也 - Means 'the mentor was the one who reallocated the papers to Mr. Yang's room,' clarifying the mentor's role.
  • 不受拨 - Means 'did not accept reallocation,' indicating a refusal to participate in the practice.
  • 一房仅中七卷 - Means 'only seven papers were selected in one room,' highlighting the low number of selections.
  • 静儒前辈本房第一 - Means 'Mr. Jingru was the top candidate in his room,' indicating his high ranking.
  • 为第二十名 - Means 'he became the twentieth-ranked candidate,' showing his final position.
  • 王铭锡竟无魁选 - Means 'Mr. Wang Mingxi did not even get selected as the top candidate,' indicating his failure to achieve the highest rank.
  • 一房两魁 - Means 'two top candidates in one room,' referring to the occurrence of two top-ranked candidates in the same room.
  • 所刻同门卷 - Means 'the collected exam papers published,' referring to the compilation and publication of exam papers.
  • 余皆尝亲见也 - Means 'I have all seen them personally,' indicating the author's firsthand experience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 科场 - Refers to the examination hall, a crucial institution in the imperial examination system.
  • 国家取人材 - Refers to the state's selection of talents, emphasizing the public and official purpose of the exams.
  • 试官 - Refers to the examiners, officials responsible for evaluating the exam papers.
  • 门生 - Refers to disciples or students, a term often used in the context of the imperial examination system.
  • 拨房 - Refers to the practice of reallocating exam papers among examiners to ensure fairness.
  • 雍正癸丑会试 - Refers to the imperial examination held in 1733 during the reign of Emperor Yongzheng.
  • 杨丈 - Refers to Mr. Yang, a respected examiner.
  • 椿 - The name of Mr. Yang.
  • 姚安公 - Refers to a respected official, possibly a mentor or senior figure.
  • 乾隆壬戌会试 - Refers to the imperial examination held in 1772 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong.
  • 诸襄七前辈 - Refers to Mr. Zhu Xiangqi, a respected examiner.
  • 总裁 - Refers to the chief examiner or the highest-ranking official overseeing the examination.
  • 戊辰会试 - Refers to the imperial examination held in 1778 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong.
  • 朱石君前辈 - Refers to Mr. Zhu Shijun, a respected examiner.
  • 汤药罔前辈 - Refers to Mr. Tang Yaowang, a respected examiner.
  • 金雨叔前辈 - Refers to Mr. Jin Yushu, a respected examiner.
  • 王铭锡 - Refers to Mr. Wang Mingxi, a candidate in the examination.
  • 任钓台前辈 - Refers to Mr. Ren DiaoTai, a respected examiner.

Proper Nouns

  • 科场 - Examination hall.
  • 国家 - State.
  • 试官 - Examiners.
  • 门生 - Disciples or students.
  • 拨房 - Reallocating exam papers.
  • 雍正 - Emperor Yongzheng.
  • 癸丑 - 1733.
  • 杨丈 - Mr. Yang.
  • 椿 - Mr. Yang's name.
  • 姚安公 - A respected official.

  • 壬戌 - 1772.

  • 戊辰 - 1778.

  • 汤药罔前辈 - Mr. Tang Yaowang.
  • 金雨叔前辈 - Mr. Jin Yushu.
  • 王铭锡 - Mr. Wang Mingxi.
